title: Kinetic Energy Transfer Lab
description: A physics simulation where the Xbot character stands on a platform connected to the InterpolationTest object via an energy conduit. The user manipulates energy levels across multiple coupled channels (translation, rotation, scale) to transfer kinetic energy between the two objects while conserving total system energy. An undo/redo timeline lets users step back through their energy allocation history.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a Kinetic Energy Transfer Lab simulation. Load Xbot.glb on the left side and InterpolationTest.glb on the right side of the scene, connected by a glowing energy conduit beam (a cyan emissive cylinder). Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 2, 6) looking at origin, with ambient light intensity 0.4 and a directional light at (5, 10, 5) intensity 1.0. Add OrbitControls. The simulation has a total conserved energy of 100 units distributed across three coupled channels: Translation (T), Rotation (R), and Scale (S). Display three vertical slider controls on the left panel labeled T, R, S for the Xbot, each ranging 0-100. The InterpolationTest object automatically receives the remaining energy per channel (conservation law: Xbot_channel + InterpolationTest_channel = channel_max). Each channel's allocated energy drives the corresponding animation: Translation energy makes the object bob up/down with amplitude proportional to energy, Rotation energy spins the object around Y-axis with speed proportional to energy, Scale energy pulses the object scale between 0.8 and 1.2 with frequency proportional to energy. Add a master energy budget slider (0-100) that caps the total energy across all three Xbot channels combined, requiring the user to balance allocations. When a slider would exceed the budget, auto-reduce the last-changed slider. Implement a full undo/redo system: every slider change pushes state to a history stack. Add Undo and Redo buttons that restore previous slider configurations. Display a real-time energy dashboard showing per-object per-channel energy values and total system energy (must always equal 300: 100 per channel). The interaction steps are: adjust T slider, adjust R slider, adjust S slider, modify master budget, rebalance T, rebalance R, undo twice, redo once, set new budget, adjust S, verify conservation readout, fine-tune R. Use AnimationMixer for any embedded GLB animations blended with procedural transforms. Add a grid helper floor and subtle fog for depth.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  xbotTranslationEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to Xbot translation channel (0-100)
  xbotRotationEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to Xbot rotation channel (0-100)
  xbotScaleEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to Xbot scale channel (0-100)
  interpolationTranslationEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to InterpolationTest translation channel (0-100)
  interpolationRotationEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to InterpolationTest rotation channel (0-100)
  interpolationScaleEnergy: number,         // energy allocated to InterpolationTest scale channel (0-100)
  totalEnergyConserved: number,         // total energy in system, should remain constant at 300
  xbotPositionY: number,         // current Y position of Xbot model
  interpolationPositionY: number,         // current Y position of InterpolationTest model
  xbotRotationY: number,         // current Y rotation of Xbot in radians
  interpolationRotationY: number,         // current Y rotation of InterpolationTest in radians
  conduitOpacity: number,         // opacity of energy conduit beam (0-1)
}
